citations.description: Ticket JSON: `"comments": []` — no comments or artifacts recording the repair of reversed links in live Jira.

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] the ~96 reversed `blocks` links (and any c8ed-reversed depends_on) are repaired in live Jira.
findings.detail: This criterion is operator-attested, requiring a concrete attestation recorded in the ticket (a reference id/URL, named actor, observed outcome, or timestamp). The ticket has zero comments (`"comments": []`), and no such attestation exists anywhere in the ticket body or metadata. The criterion is not met.
findings.remediation: Record proof as a ticket comment naming: (1) the reconcile run or repair script reference/id, (2) the observed outcome (e.g. '96 Blocks links corrected, 0 depends_on reversed links found'), and (3) the date/time the repair was confirmed (e.g. 2026-07-16 run log URL or output excerpt).

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
